name: Tossal de Sant Donat and L'Aguda Loop
name_original: L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà
description: Explore the L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat circular hike from Torà, a moderate 4.6-mile route through agricultural landscapes and historical s
region_intro: Hiking around Torà offers diverse outdoor experiences within the Solsonès comarca of Lleida, Catalonia. The region is characterized by rolling hills, verdant valleys, and flowing waterways, providing a varied natural landscape for exploration. Prominent hills such as el Tossal de l'Aguda and el Tossal de Sant Donat define the northern sector, while river valleys like la Riera de Llanera shape the terrain. This topography supports a network of hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Torà The most popular hiking route is L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding hilly terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Castellfollit y Sants Metges de Marçà — circular desde el Llobregós, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail follows the Llobregós river, offering serene riparian scenery. Local hikers also love the Balsa de Alzina from Santuario del Miracle, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Torà is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and diverse natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate climbs. The routes in Torà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Torà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Torà?
A: Torà is characterized by a diverse landscape of rolling hills, verdant valleys, and flowing waterways. You'll find prominent hills like &lt;strong&gt;el Tossal de l'Aguda&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;el Tossal de Sant Donat&lt;/strong&gt; in the north, offering rewarding vistas. River valleys, such as &lt;strong&gt;la Riera de Llanera&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Llobregós River&lt;/strong&gt;, also shape the terrain, providing serene paths along watercourses.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Torà?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes around Torà are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133924054/l-aguda-y-serra-de-san-donat-circular-desde-tora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.3 km loop. Another option is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133917976/castillo-de-castellfollit-y-sants-metges-de-marca-circular-desde-el-llobregos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Castellfollit y Sants Metges de Marçà — circular desde el Llobregós&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Llobregós river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torà?
A: Spring and fall are particularly pleasant seasons for hiking in Torà. The weather is generally mild, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant, offering comfortable conditions for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Torà?
A: Yes, Torà offers hiking routes suitable for various skill levels, including easy walks that are great for families. There are 16 easy routes available, such as the 5.6 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133917976/castillo-de-castellfollit-y-sants-metges-de-marca-circular-desde-el-llobregos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Castellfollit y Sants Metges de Marçà — circular desde el Llobregós&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a gentle path along the Llobregós river.

Q: What kind of historical landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: The region around Torà is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can explore various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Boixadors&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5121902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Church of Sant Martí de Riner&lt;/a&gt;. Religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5054493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of El Miracle&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Torà?
A: Hikes in Torà vary in length and duration. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133924054/l-aguda-y-serra-de-san-donat-circular-desde-tora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà&lt;/a&gt; is about 7.3 km and takes around 2 hours 23 minutes. An easy option like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133917976/castillo-de-castellfollit-y-sants-metges-de-marca-circular-desde-el-llobregos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Castellfollit y Sants Metges de Marçà — circular desde el Llobregós&lt;/a&gt; is 5.6 km and typically takes about 1 hour 36 minutes.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Torà?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails available around Torà, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. This includes 16 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Torà?
A: The hiking trails in Torà are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the picturesque views, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the surrounding countryside?
A: Absolutely. The hilly terrain, particularly areas like &lt;strong&gt;el Tossal de l'Aguda&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;el Tossal de Sant Donat&lt;/strong&gt;, provides excellent opportunities for panoramic views. Routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133924054/l-aguda-y-serra-de-san-donat-circular-desde-tora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà&lt;/a&gt; are known for offering views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or creeks?
A: Yes, the landscape of Torà is shaped by several water bodies, including &lt;strong&gt;la Riera de Llanera&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Llobregós River&lt;/strong&gt;. You can find serene paths that follow these watercourses, offering a different kind of natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133917976/castillo-de-castellfollit-y-sants-metges-de-marca-circular-desde-el-llobregos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Castellfollit y Sants Metges de Marçà — circular desde el Llobregós&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, follows the Llobregós river.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on trails in Torà?
A: The elevation gain varies depending on the trail's difficulty. Moderate routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1133924054/l-aguda-y-serra-de-san-donat-circular-desde-tora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat — circular desde Torà&lt;/a&gt;, can have an elevation gain of around 310 meters. Easier trails will have less, while more challenging routes will feature greater ascents.

faqs.question: Where does the L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat circular hike start?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Torà. While specific parking details aren't provided, Torà is the natural starting point for this local trail.

faqs.question: How long does this circular hike typically take?
faqs.answer: The hike is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete, covering a distance of about 7.3 kilometers.

faqs.question: How difficult is the L'Aguda y Serra de San Donat trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as moderate. However, local research suggests it is generally considered easy and suitable for most fitness levels, making it accessible to a wide range of hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through the agricultural landscape around Torà, characterized by dryland farming and cereal fields. You'll enjoy natural surroundings and have the opportunity to explore some of the area's historical sites.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover several points of interest, including the Statue of the Virgin Mary (Creu de l'Aguda), the Sant Salvador del Coll de l'Aguda Hermitage, and the Sant Donat Hermitage. The Convent of Saint Anthony of Padua of Torà is also nearby.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific leash rules are not detailed, trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the agricultural landscape, spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during that season.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access this trail.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails, including 'Aguda, Biosca, Torà', 'La Vall del Llobregós.Variant Torà - Mas Mesquita', and 'El Camí IT2TR3 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat'.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Torà?
faqs.answer: Yes, Torà is a municipality with a rich architectural heritage, and you can expect to find local amenities such as cafes and pubs in the town center before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, given its generally easy rating and accessible terrain, this circular route is suitable for families looking for a pleasant walk through natural and historical surroundings.
